title: UHID lookup can autofill stale patient data after the field is cleared
reasoning: In-flight UHID requests are only invalidated when fetchPatientByUhid starts a newer valid request. If a user enters a valid UHID, the request starts, and then the user clears or shortens the UHID below three characters before the response returns, handleUhidChange clears only the debounce timer and does not increment latestRequestId. The old response still passes the requestId check and can populate first_name, last_name, DOB, phone, and email for a UHID that is no longer present in the form.
reproduction: Type a valid UHID and wait until the lookup request is sent. Before the response returns, delete the UHID field or reduce it to fewer than three characters. When the original request resolves, the patient details are still written into the form.
recommendation: Invalidate any active lookup when the UHID value becomes too short or empty, and clear the loading state for that path. For example, increment latestRequestId.current in handleUhidChange before returning for values below the minimum length.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are included for the step components or for UHID lookup timing behavior.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a component test that starts a UHID lookup, changes the field to an empty value before resolving the mocked API promise, then verifies setValue is not called with patient details.
minimumFixScope: Step1PatientInfo UHID change handling and stale request invalidation.

history.reasoning: The original evidence path src/pages/steps/Step1PatientInfo.jsx still exists, but that specific handler has been changed: handleUhidChange now increments latestRequestId.current and calls setUhidLoading(false) when value.trim().length < 3, so the original Step1 stale-response path is fixed. However the same UHID stale autofill issue remains elsewhere in the current repository. In src/components/PatientIntakeForm.jsx, handleUhidChange returns on cleared UHID at line 84 without incrementing uhidRequestId.current; an already-started lookup from lines 95-106 can still pass the requestId check and set patient fields. In src/pages/LeadBox.jsx, handleUhidChange starts api.getLeadByUhid at lines 378-382 and writes patient data at lines 385-397 with no request id or invalidation when the UHID is cleared/shortened. No test/spec files were found, package.json has no test script, and npm run lint was rejected by policy in this read-only session.
